mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344895M as of April 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This constrains equity valuations and reduces forward returns over 60 trading days as higher discount rates compress present values of future cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.79% as of April 2005, approaching tightening threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which increases corporate costs and reduces profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% and PAYEMS at 133293K as of April 2005
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When both UNRATE < 5.5% and PAYEMS growth > 150K/month, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above trend indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This supports corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets react to supply strength.
regime_hint: expansion phase with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth > 0.5% MoM, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand destruction. This reduces construction employment, supplier revenues, and consumer wealth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1837K units as of March 2005
failure_condition: When HOUST declines > 10% from 3MA, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals the Fed will maintain or increase tightening pressure. This reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 193.2 and FEDFUNDS at 2.79% as of March-April 2005
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 3% annualized signals robust economic expansion and earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11078.2B (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2004
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth > 3.0% annualized, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in < 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
